tavily.answer: Ethiopia has experienced significant international displacement due to ongoing conflicts, particularly in regions like Tigray, Oromia, and Amhara. The country's first nationwide elections since the end of the Tigray war in 2022 saw the ruling party retain its majority, but the Tigray region was excluded from participating. The United States lifted an arms embargo on Ethiopia, which has raised regional security concerns, especially regarding Ethiopia's ambitions for Red Sea access. Additionally, Ethiopia's involvement in Sudan's conflict has sparked fears of regional destabilization. The number of internal displacements in Ethiopia remains high, with over 7,400 attacks recorded since 2022, according to Armed Conflict Location & Event Data (ACLED).

items.title: At least 30 dead in Congo camp as Ebola outbreak raises alarm - Honolulu Star-Advertiser
items.snippet: A health worker in personal protective equipment stands near displaced people waiting for the burial of Ebola victims at the Kigonze displaced persons camp, one month after an outbreak was declared, in Bunia, eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, on Thursday.
